params_frozen.rationale: Tests whether v1's Stage 1 regime classifier added value or was overhead. v1's per-regime architecture saw 3 of 4 regimes (Q2/Q3/Q4) shrink to all-zero coefs — only Q1_calm fitted real signal. v2 keeps the same 21-feature whitelist + same target + same λ procedure, but fits ONE global Lasso on the entire train pool with no regime conditioning. Single architectural variable change → clean attribution.

stage1_regime_classifier.note: v1's Stage 1 (VIX-quartile rolling-252d thresholds) explicitly removed. Regime info NOT exposed to model — no one-hot encoding, no regime feature. Pure ablation.

stage2_conditional_alpha.regularization: L1 with α selected via 3-fold expanding-window nested CV on full train_pool with 60-day embargo (same procedure as v1, applied once instead of per-regime)
stage2_conditional_alpha.fit_strategy: single global model fitted on entire train pool

params_frozen.feature_pit_provenance: Identical to v1 (multi_source_two_stage_2026_04_30) — features.py and target.py reused without modification. F4 fix + O2 invariant remain locked by tests/test_pit_insider_delisting_exclusion.py and tests/test_pit_universe_delisted_inclusion.py.

target_definition.construction: raw excess return over RF (5d-forward close-to-close minus 5d-cumulative RF) — IDENTICAL to v1, reused via target.forward_excess_return
target_definition.post_hoc_attribution: Carhart-4F regression of realised portfolio returns on Mkt-RF/SMB/HML/MOM. NOT pre-residualized at target level (Gu-Kelly-Xiu 2024). Carhart factor cache vintage matches v1's run (2026-03-27 download, coverage through 2026-02-27) for apples-to-apples comparability.

portfolio_construction.ranking: predicted forward excess return per (ticker, asof) from the global Lasso
portfolio_construction.universe: AlphaLens PIT universe (yfinance + survivorship parquet), ADV ≥ $5M filter applied at scoring time

cost_model.applied_to: every entry and exit (round-trip = 20 bps base + 5 bps adverse selection one-way → 30 bps total)

anti_overfit_constraints.no_post_hoc_feature_addition: any new feature requires a new pre-registration with its own Bonferroni denominator
anti_overfit_constraints.no_post_hoc_feature_removal: v1's zero-coef finding does NOT license dropping features in v2 — that would be HARKing on v1's holdout result

params_frozen.differences_from_v1: ONLY Stage 1 architecture is removed. Features, target, λ procedure, embargo, top_n, weighting, rebalance cadence, cost model, universe — all identical. This is a single-variable architectural ablation.

success_criteria.primary_metric: Carhart-4F α t-stat on holdout 2024-04-30 → 2026-04-30 portfolio returns
success_criteria.pass_rule: Holdout Carhart-4F α t-stat ≥ 2.24 AND holdout Sharpe net ≥ 0.5 AND holdout α annualized ≥ 3% AND holdout MaxDD ≥ -35%
success_criteria.mid_rule: α t-stat ≥ 1.5 AND Sharpe net ≥ 0.3 → MID; refine + re-pre-register before any deploy
success_criteria.fail_rule: α t-stat < 1.5 OR Sharpe net < 0.3 → FAIL, log to ledger, document architecture verdict
success_criteria.phase_robustness_followup: If holdout PASS or MID, run multi-phase audit (5 phase offsets) on 2014-2026 with same architecture; PASS only if every phase α t-stat ≥ 1.5 AND mean α t-stat ≥ 2.24. If holdout FAIL outright, skip Phase C (saves ~85min compute) and log FAIL directly.
success_criteria.comparison_to_v1: v1 (regime-conditional) had mean phase αt=+0.65 (±0.91), excess net 0.0% (±4.9pp). v2 architecture verdict will be: BETTER (Stage 1 hurt v1) if v2 mean αt > 0.65; SAME (Stage 1 was noise) if within ±0.3 of v1; WORSE (Stage 1 helped) if v2 mean αt < 0.35. This comparison is descriptive, NOT a pre-reg gate — gate is the Bonferroni 2.24.
